raw_consumer_statement:
Re : Case ID : XXXX I am filing a complaint against Citi for failing to conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act. Citi denied my dispute for a {$250.00} 'cleaning fee ' from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX simply because the merchant provided a signed contract.

Citi completely ignored the physical evidence I provided showing the merchant 's 'damage report ' was dated 5 days AFTER I returned the car, and the odometer had increased by 5 miles. A signed contract does not give a merchant a blank check to charge me for damage discovered a week after the vehicle left my custody. Furthermore, the merchant 's photos show only light, vacuumable sand ( normal wear-and-tear for a Florida beach rental ) , yet they deceptively included 4 different photos of the exact same small cargo area to inflate the appearance of damage.

Citi 's dispute team refused to look at the dates, mileage, or deceptive photos, blindly sided with the merchant, and a supervisor refused to escalate my appeal, blaming XXXX rules. I want this decision reversed and my account credited.

Note on Attached Documents : 1 ) XXXX  Damage Capture Report : Proves the inspection was done 5 days after return, the car was driven an additional 5 miles while in XXXXXXXX XXXX custody, and shows the redundant photos padding the claim. 

2 ) XXXX  XXXX  Agreement ( Receipt ) : Proves XXXXXXXX XXXX system recorded an erroneous starting mileage of 200 and claims a total driven distance of 3,219 miles.

3 ) XXXX Credit Card Statement & XXXX Receipt : I am attaching these to irrefutably prove my family 's location. These documents show we stayed locally in XXXX, FL for the entire duration of the trip. This proves it is physically impossible for us to have driven the 3,219 miles that XXXX  erroneous paperwork implies ( over 500 miles a day ). This proves XXXX 's documentation of the vehicle 's condition is entirely falsified and unreliable.
